name: Motilal Oswal Nifty India Defence ETF

index.name: Nifty India Defence Index TRI
index.description: Nifty India Defence Index tracks companies engaged in defence manufacturing — a emerging ₹10-15K Cr addressable market growing 15%+ annually. Government of India defence budget: ₹6.21 Lakh Cr (FY25), targeting 3-4% annual growth. Structural tailwind: India Device Designation (IDDM) policy mandates 75% defence procurement from domestic industry (vs 5-10% historically), creating Protected market where imported weapons are barred. India Defence Exports: targeting $5B by 2025 (vs ₹1,000 Cr currently) via license manufacturing of global platforms locally (e.g., HAL manufacturing Tejas fighters locally vs ₹150 Cr unit cost). Supply chain shift: post-geopolitical tensions (Ukraine, Taiwan strait) global powers requiring allied manufacturing bases — India emerging as third manufacturing hub alongside US/NATO allies. Four subsectors: (1) Aerospace (HAL): ₹90K Cr orderbook, 7+ year visibility; (2) Defence Electronics (BEL): radars, missiles, ₹65K Cr order backlog; (3) Shipbuilding (Mazagon Dock, GRSE): submarines, frigates; (4) Ammunition (Solar Industries, Bharat Forge): artillery shells, missiles. Capital intensity moderate (₹500-1000 Cr capex for new facilities). Profit margins: 15-25% EBITDA, improving as scale increases. Geopolitical risk: if tensions ease, defence orders decline; if conflicts intensify (South China Sea, Russia dynamics), ordering accelerates.

holdings.name: Bharat Electronics
holdings.description: Leading defence electronics PSU. Major supplier of radars, missile systems, and communication equipment.

holdings.name: Hindustan Aeronautics
holdings.description: India's premier aerospace manufacturer. Key supplier of aircraft and helicopters to Indian defence forces.

holdings.name: Bharat Forge
holdings.description: Major player in artillery, armoured vehicles, and munitions. Strong defence order book.

holdings.name: Solar Industries India
holdings.description: Largest manufacturer of industrial explosives and defence ammunition in India.

holdings.name: Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders
holdings.description: Leading naval shipbuilder. Monopoly in submarine building for Indian Navy.

holdings.name: Bharat Dynamics
holdings.description: Key manufacturer of missiles and allied defence equipment.

holdings.name: Cochin Shipyard
holdings.description: Major shipbuilder for Indian Navy and Coast Guard.

holdings.name: Astra Microwave Products
holdings.description: Specialist in RF and microwave products for defence and space.

holdings.name: Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers
holdings.description: Kolkata-based shipbuilder. First Indian shipyard to export warships.

holdings.name: Data Patterns (India)
holdings.description: Defence and aerospace electronics company. Works on radar, avionics, and missile systems.

index.name: Nifty India Defence Index
index.description: Tracks companies engaged in Indian defence manufacturing and services. India's defence budget stands at ₹6.21 lakh crore in FY25. Indigenization mandate requires 75% of defence procurement from domestic industry under IDDM policy. India targets $5B in defence exports by 2025. Massive multi-year order backlogs provide unprecedented revenue visibility for both PSU and private defence players.

holdings.name: HAL (Hindustan Aeronautics)
holdings.description: India's only aerospace manufacturing PSU. Makes Tejas LCA Mk1A fighter jets (83 aircraft ordered), ALH Dhruv helicopters, Dornier aircraft and servicing of MiG-21, Sukhoi-30 fleets. Order backlog of ₹90,000+ Cr from IAF, Army and Navy with 7+ year visibility.

holdings.name: BEL (Bharat Electronics)
holdings.description: Defence electronics PSU making Akash missile radar systems, submarine sonar, BMS (Battlefield Management System), electronic warfare and communication equipment. ₹65,000+ Cr order backlog. Exports to 20+ countries.

holdings.name: L&T
holdings.description: L&T's defence division excels in naval systems, armoured vehicles, K9 Vajra self-propelled howitzers, and NGARM anti-radiation missiles with DRDO. Building India's first private-sector submarine under Project-75 India Scorpene variant.

holdings.name: Bharat Forge
holdings.description: World's 2nd largest forging company now a serious defence player. Makes ATAGS 155mm/52-calibre artillery guns, armoured vehicle hulls, munitions and aerospace structural components. 20% revenue from defence with aggressive growth plans.

holdings.name: Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders
holdings.description: Mumbai-based premier naval shipbuilder. Building Project-75 Scorpene submarines (6 delivered, more contracted), Project-15B destroyers and stealth frigates. ₹50,000+ Cr orderbook with 5+ year revenue visibility. Monopoly in submarine building.

holdings.name: Garden Reach Shipbuilders
holdings.description: Kolkata-based naval shipbuilder. Builds frigates (Project-17A), survey vessels, landing craft and fast patrol vessels. First Indian shipyard to export warships — delivered vessels to Mauritius (CGS Barracuda) and Bangladesh Navy.

holdings.name: BEML Ltd
holdings.description: Makes metro rail cars (Mumbai, Delhi, Bengaluru, Pune), mining equipment (dumpers, draglines) and high-mobility vehicles for Indian Army. Exporting metro coaches to Singapore MRT. Also makes helicopter ground support equipment.

holdings.name: Paras Defence
holdings.description: Niche defence-tech company. Makes electro-optic systems for missiles and helicopters, star trackers for ISRO/DRDO satellites, image intensifier tubes for night-vision goggles. India's only manufacturer of several critical optics components.

holdings.name: Data Patterns
holdings.description: Chennai-based defence and aerospace electronics company. Makes radar signal processing systems, avionics, missile seekers and electronic warfare modules. Works on DRDO programs including Tejas, Pinaka and Astra missile systems.

holdings.name: Solar Industries India
holdings.description: India's largest manufacturer of industrial explosives and defence ammunition. Making 155mm artillery shells, hand grenades, rockets and warheads. Sole supplier to Indian Army for several warhead types. Massive export orders from the US and Europe post-Ukraine conflict.

name: Groww Mutual Fund - Groww Nifty India Defence ETF

index.name: Nifty India Defence Index- Total Return Index
index.description: Nifty India Defence Index tracks listed defence-sector companies designated by the Ministry of Defence. India's defence budget crossed ₹6.2 lakh crore in FY26 with 25%+ earmarked for domestic procurement (Make in India defence). HAL, BEL, BDL, L&T Defence lead public + private sector collaboration. Export target: $5B by 2025 (from $0.2B in 2014). Rising geopolitical tensions (China, Pakistan border) drive sustained multi-decade capex; DRDO pipeline of 400+ indigenous systems under development.

holdings.name: Hindustan Aeronautics (HAL)
holdings.description: India's premier aircraft manufacturer — produces Tejas, Dhruv helicopter, Advanced Light Helicopter and Su-30MKI wings. ₹94,000 Cr order book (8-year revenue visibility). AMCA (Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft) and TEDBF (carrier fighter) in development. Navratna PSU with MRO and engine manufacturing expansion.

holdings.name: Larsen & Toubro
holdings.description: India's largest defence private sector player. K9 Vajra howitzers (200+ delivered to Army), Shakti next-gen missile boat warships (Navy), VSHORADS MANPADS (Air Force). Kaveri Marine engine project. L&T Defence subsidiary targeting ₹30,000 Cr revenues.

holdings.name: Bharat Electronics (BEL)
holdings.description: India's premier defence electronics PSU. Central Acquisition Radar, BEL-LRDE radar systems, Battle Management Systems, Electronic Warfare (Shakti jammer). ₹68,000 Cr order book includes MRASHM (anti-ship) and Astra Beyond-Visual-Range missile electronics.

holdings.name: Bharat Dynamics (BDL)
holdings.description: India's sole guided missile manufacturer for all three services. Akash SAM, Konkurs ATGM, Varunastra torpedo, Helina helicopter-launched missile. QRSAM (Quick Reaction SAM) and MRSAM naval variant production ramping. Export orders from Southeast Asia.

holdings.name: Cochin Shipyard
holdings.description: India's largest shipbuilder by tonnage. INS Vikrant (first indigenous aircraft carrier) delivered. Next generation destroyers (P-17B) under construction for Navy. Commercial shipbuilding for international LNG carriers, dredgers. Green ship (LNG, hydrogen) technology future focus.

holdings.name: Data Patterns (India)
holdings.description: Listed defence electronics MSME — radars, missile seekers, electronic warfare kits, avionics. Tier-1 supplier to HAL, DRDO, BEL, ISRO. Every Tejas fighter carries Data Patterns avionics components. ASH radar for UAVs, Leonardo radar countermeasures.

holdings.name: MTAR Technologies
holdings.description: Critical precision components for nuclear, space and defence — ball screws, roller screws, actuators. ISRO's primary supplier for PSLV/GSLV/LVM3 launch vehiclecomponents. NASA/SpaceX supply chain entry. Agni-V ICBM actuators system sole supplier.
